shared.module_boundary_note: NDYRA Core keeps public acquisition, billing truth, entitlements, and admin wiring surfaces. BizGym owns gym-ops runtime. Timer runtime remains separate.

surfaces.title: Business Boundary
surfaces.summary: This shell stays in NDYRA Core so public acquisition, billing truth, and admin wiring remain coherent while gym-ops runtime continues to live in BizGym.
surfaces.ownership: NDYRA Core owns public for-gyms acquisition, business billing truth, and live wiring/admin status. BizGym owns operational check-in, schedule, settings, migrations, and day-to-day gym tools.
available_now: Public acquisition is live under /for-gyms/, /for-gyms/pricing.html, and /for-gyms/start.html.
Business billing truth stays here at /biz/account/ and reflects runtime/config readiness honestly.
Admin deployment truth stays here at /admin/status/, /admin/wiring/, and /admin/execute/.
owned_elsewhere: Gym settings, kiosk controls, waivers, and migration commit/cutover flows
next_steps: Use the public for-gyms flow to stage acquisition and billing setup.
Use /admin/execute/ to run the deployment confidence checklist and release closeout packet.
Use the BizGym handoff docs when gym-ops runtime work resumes.

surfaces.title: Check-In Boundary
surfaces.summary: Check-In remains paused in NDYRA Core and intentionally stops at the shared routing shell. The real operational flow continues to belong to BizGym.
surfaces.ownership: Shared platform/backend spine is acknowledged here, but member/business runtime for check-in is not duplicated in Core.
available_now: Billing truth and deployment readiness remain available through /biz/account/ and /admin/execute/.
Public gym acquisition remains available through /for-gyms/ and /gym/:slug/join.
owned_elsewhere: Live issue radar and staff overrides
next_steps: Keep Check-In paused in Core until explicitly resumed.
Use the BizGym module contract before changing any check-in behavior.

surfaces.title: Check-In Kiosk Boundary
surfaces.summary: The kiosk route is preserved so IA and rewrites stay stable, but kiosk runtime is intentionally not forked into NDYRA Core.
surfaces.ownership: BizGym owns the kiosk experience, front-desk flow, and waiver/membership guardrails.
available_now: The route is present for QA, architecture continuity, and future reintegration.
Core still exposes plan/billing truth and deployment readiness elsewhere.
owned_elsewhere: Operational audit trail for staff actions
next_steps: Verify route presence only; do not treat this as active kiosk runtime.
Refer to the BizGym handoff before adding kiosk behavior.

surfaces.title: Live Check-In Boundary
surfaces.summary: This route preserves the live queue entry point without pretending the live issue radar or override system exists in Core.
surfaces.ownership: BizGym owns the live queue, staff overrides, and issue management.
available_now: The route remains for architecture continuity and QA navigation.
Core still carries billing truth, acquisition, and admin deployment controls.
owned_elsewhere: Issue triage and staff override tools
next_steps: Keep this route as a boundary shell unless Check-In is explicitly resumed here.
Use /admin/execute/ for deployment readiness, not this page, to judge launch state.

surfaces.title: Schedule Boundary
surfaces.summary: Schedule operations are intentionally left with BizGym so NDYRA Core does not fork a second staff scheduling engine.
surfaces.ownership: BizGym owns class/session operations, capacity, staff assignment, and schedule management.
available_now: Public gym discovery and member event surfaces stay available in Core.
Business billing truth and acquisition flows remain available in Core.
next_steps: Use member-facing event and public gym surfaces here in Core.
Use the BizGym contract for any future schedule ops work.

surfaces.title: Settings Boundary
surfaces.summary: Business settings stay intentionally routed but not reimplemented here. NDYRA Core does not fork staff, kiosk, waiver, or payout settings from BizGym.
surfaces.ownership: BizGym owns gym settings, staff management, kiosks, waivers, payouts, and token-rule operations.
available_now: Public acquisition and plan selection remain in Core.
Business billing truth stays in /biz/account/.
next_steps: Use /for-gyms/start.html to stage a plan selection.
Use BizGym docs when settings runtime resumes.

surfaces.title: Migration Boundary
surfaces.summary: Migration routing stays visible in Core so the architecture and deploy story remain coherent, but import/verify/commit/cutover runtime is not duplicated here.
surfaces.ownership: BizGym owns migration tooling. NDYRA Core only preserves the IA shell, live wiring truth, and billing/admin context around it.
available_now: Admin live execution and wiring truth remain available here for deployment prep.
Public acquisition can still onboard gyms before migration runtime resumes.
next_steps: Use these routes as QA-visible placeholders for architecture continuity only.
Use /admin/execute/ and the release closeout packet to track real deployment progress.

surfaces.title: Migration Members Boundary
surfaces.summary: Member import logic is intentionally held out of Core. This route exists so the migration IA and routing map stay stable until BizGym runtime is attached.
surfaces.ownership: BizGym owns member import, normalization, and QA reconciliation flows.
available_now: Business acquisition, billing truth, and deployment/admin truth remain live in Core.
next_steps: Do not treat this route as an active import tool.
Use the BizGym contract before reactivating import behavior.

surfaces.title: Migration Schedule Boundary
surfaces.summary: Schedule import remains a boundary shell in Core so route continuity survives without forking schedule-ops logic out of BizGym.
surfaces.ownership: BizGym owns schedule import, mapping, and verification flows.
available_now: Public gym profiles and member events remain available in Core.
next_steps: Use this page as a route-presence shell only.
Use the BizGym handoff before enabling any schedule import actions.

surfaces.title: Migration Verify Boundary
surfaces.summary: Verification remains part of the BizGym migration toolchain. NDYRA Core keeps the route but does not invent a second verification engine.
surfaces.ownership: BizGym owns data verification, exception handling, and operator approval steps.
available_now: Live wiring/admin truth and public acquisition remain active in Core.
owned_elsewhere: Operator approvals tied to migration execution
next_steps: Track deployment progress through /admin/execute/, not through this placeholder route.
Use BizGym migration docs for runtime behavior.

surfaces.title: Migration Commit Boundary
surfaces.summary: Commit/cutover actions remain intentionally out of Core. This page preserves the route without pretending the irreversible migration step is active here.
surfaces.ownership: BizGym owns commit execution and operational safeguards.
available_now: Admin execution truth and business billing truth remain available in Core.
next_steps: Treat this as a route-presence shell only.
Use the release closeout packet for deployment evidence, not this page.

surfaces.title: Migration Cutover Boundary
surfaces.summary: Cutover remains a protected BizGym runtime concern. NDYRA Core preserves the route for continuity but does not imitate the actual cutover flow.
surfaces.ownership: BizGym owns cutover, rollback readiness, and operator-run release sequencing.
available_now: Admin execute and the release closeout packet remain available for deployment evidence capture.
owned_elsewhere: Rollback readiness and final operator checks
next_steps: Keep this route as a boundary shell until BizGym runtime is attached.
Use /admin/execute/ plus the release closeout packet to drive launch evidence.

surfaces.title: Gym Timer Boundary
surfaces.summary: The shared gym-timer route stays visible, but NDYRA Core does not fork the separate Timer module runtime. Core only carries timer entitlements and member-side library access.
surfaces.ownership: Timer runtime and gym timer authoring remain separate. NDYRA Core owns entitlements, catalog visibility, wallet/purchases, and member library unlocks.
available_now: Members can access owned timer content through /app/library/timers/.
Public/member marketplace surfaces remain in Core.
next_steps: Use member-side timer library and wallet/purchases here in Core.
Do not interpret this page as the timer engine itself.

surfaces.title: Gym Timer Builder Boundary
surfaces.summary: Builder authoring remains intentionally outside NDYRA Core. This route preserves navigation continuity without duplicating the Timer module.
surfaces.ownership: Timer module owns builder runtime. NDYRA Core owns entitlements, purchases, and member library access around it.
available_now: Timer purchases and entitlements still surface through wallet, shop, and timer library in Core.
owned_elsewhere: Authoring controls and gym-timer publish flow
next_steps: Use the separate Timer module for builder runtime when attached.
Use Core only for acquisition, ownership, and unlock state.

surfaces.title: Biz Shop Boundary
surfaces.summary: Seller-side shop management remains a BizGym concern. NDYRA Core already owns public pricing, checkout truth, entitlements, purchases, and member-side marketplace access.
surfaces.ownership: NDYRA Core owns acquisition, checkout readiness truth, wallet/purchases, and member library unlocks. BizGym owns seller-side product ops and shop management.
available_now: Members can browse and purchase through /app/shop/, /app/wallet/, and /app/purchases/.
Public/business acquisition and plan selection remain live in Core.
next_steps: Use /biz/account/ to verify billing runtime truth.
Use BizGym docs before extending seller-side operations here.

surfaces.title: Biz Timer Packs Boundary
surfaces.summary: Timer-pack selling remains seller-side ops and therefore stays out of Core runtime. Core already carries purchase, entitlement, and member library access for owned packs.
surfaces.ownership: NDYRA Core owns timer entitlements, wallet/purchases, and member library access. Seller-side timer-pack management belongs to BizGym plus the separate Timer module.
available_now: Members can access owned timer content and timer-related entitlements in Core.
owned_elsewhere: Pack authoring workflows tied to the Timer module
next_steps: Use Core for purchase/access truth only.
Use BizGym/Timer module docs before adding pack management runtime.

surfaces.title: Biz Moves Boundary
surfaces.summary: Move-detail ops remain a BizGym concern. NDYRA Core already carries public workouts and member-side workouts surfaces without duplicating gym-ops authoring.
surfaces.ownership: NDYRA Core owns public workouts marketing and member workout browsing. BizGym owns business-side move ops and deeper gym content tooling.
available_now: Public workouts live under /workouts/.
Member workout browsing lives under /app/workouts/.
next_steps: Use the public/member workouts surfaces in Core for current browsing flows.
Do not treat this page as a business content editor.

surfaces.title: Biz Move Detail Boundary
surfaces.summary: The business move-detail route is preserved for IA continuity but intentionally stops short of recreating business authoring/runtime inside Core.
surfaces.ownership: BizGym owns the business move detail/editor runtime. NDYRA Core keeps only public/member workout discovery surfaces.
available_now: Current workout discovery remains available in /workouts/ and /app/workouts/.
next_steps: Use this as a route-presence shell only.
Use BizGym docs before adding any authoring/runtime behavior.
